stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced May 4: consolidated net profit doubled (2x) to Rs 1,290.47 cr; total income Rs 12,553.50 cr vs Rs 9,142.64 cr YoY. FY26 PAT Rs 1,600.26 cr vs Rs 533.90 cr in FY25 (3x). Final dividend of Rs 1.40/share approved. Order book exceeds Rs 1.6 lakh cr (some sources put total at Rs 2.4 lakh cr including FY26 inflows of ~Rs 75,000 cr). Stock rallied ~44% in April, hit 52-week high of Rs 358 on April 29 from Rs 245.55 on March 30.
stocks.sector_context: Capital goods PSU theme aligned with Union Budget 2026-27 priorities (Rs 80,000 cr disinvestment target FY27, infrastructure push). Sector projected to grow ~15% CAGR. India power T&D EPC market expected to grow from $28-32 bn (FY25) to $75-88 bn by 2035. Thermal capex revival + renewables tailwind drives BHEL order inflows; PSU re-rating theme intact.
stocks.peer_comparison: Among capital goods PSUs (BEL, BEML, HAL), BHEL is the deepest turnaround story - PAT grew 3x in FY26 vs FY25. Order book of Rs 2.4 lakh cr is among largest in PSU capital goods space. Stock's 44% one-month rally outpaced most PSU peers, but valuation (post-rally) now demands flawless execution vs peers like L&T or Siemens that trade on more predictable earnings.
red_flags: Stock just hit 52-week high - extended after 44% rally in April; profit-taking risk on Q4 print
Execution risk on the Rs 2.4 lakh cr order book - working capital intensity historically high for BHEL
Long project cycles in thermal/power expose to commodity inflation and forex movements
PSU disinvestment target of Rs 80,000 cr for FY27 raises potential OFS supply overhang
catalysts: Q4 FY26 print (just reported) - 2x PAT growth is a positive trigger if not yet absorbed
Rs 1.40/share final dividend record date upcoming
FY27 order inflow guidance and analyst upgrades after strong Q4
Continued thermal capex from NTPC and state utilities; renewables order pipeline

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results scheduled May 6, 2026 - board to also consider first share BUYBACK in two years and final dividend on same date. Strong April 2026 retail/wholesale momentum: total industry two-wheeler sales +38% YoY in April. Bajaj's electric Chetak sold 32,883 units in April (+72% YoY), retaining #2 EV 2W slot behind TVS. March 2026 total sales 4,45,377 units vs 3,69,823 YoY (+20%). ICICI Securities estimates Q4 PAT +27.5% YoY to Rs 2,613 cr; revenue +29.4% to Rs 15,714 cr. Stock gained ~3% on April auto sales data.
stocks.sector_context: India two-wheeler industry in robust upcycle: April 2026 industry +38% YoY driven by rural recovery and replacement demand. EV 2W penetration accelerating - April 2026 EV 2W sales 1,48,677 units (#2 month ever). Legacy OEMs (TVS, Bajaj, Hero) command 55%+ share in EV vs early-stage startups. Export revival aided by stable EM currencies and ASEAN/LatAm demand. Margin pressure persists from raw material and freight costs.
stocks.peer_comparison: Bajaj's April EV growth (+72%) trails TVS (+96%) and Hero Vida (+148%) but on a larger base, and absolute leadership in ICE motorcycles + 3W exports remains. Eicher (Royal Enfield) reported +31% in April. Bajaj's ~Rs 2,613 cr est. Q4 PAT positions it as the most profitable two-wheeler OEM in India by absolute earnings. Buyback announcement (if approved May 6) sets it apart from peers in capital return.
red_flags: Margin compression risk: rising raw material, freight, competitive intensity may pull EBITDA margin down 14-20 bps in Q4 vs expected expansion
Stock rich on valuation - run-up over April leaves limited room if buyback size disappoints
EV market share losses to TVS/Hero Vida persist despite Chetak growth
Export demand sensitive to oil prices and EM FX volatility
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results + buyback consideration on May 6, 2026 (event 2 days away)
Final dividend recommendation on May 6
April 2026 monthly volumes already reported - strong tone for Q1FY27
GST rate rationalisation discussions on two-wheelers (potential industry tailwind)

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced April 29: consolidated PAT +64% YoY to Rs 4,271 cr; revenue Rs 14,223-15,989 cr (sources vary by consolidation basis), EBITDA +27% to Rs 6,498 cr. Power sales volume rose to 27.2 BU vs 26.4 BU YoY. FY26 PAT Rs 12,971 cr (vs Rs 12,750 cr FY25); FY26 revenue Rs 55,583 cr; total volume 99.15 BU (+3.4%). Capacity tie-ups reached 13.3 GW; secured 1,600 MW PPA from Maharashtra DISCOM under DBFOO model in Q4. Long-term capacity target raised to 41,870 MW by FY32 (from 30,670 MW); planned capex Rs 2 lakh cr.
stocks.sector_context: Indian power sector continues to benefit from peak power demand growth, thermal PPA awards, and capacity-shortage backdrop. Government's capacity addition push under National Electricity Plan favours scaled IPPs with execution capability. Coal availability stable; merchant tariffs supportive. Renewables transition still leaves 7-9 years of strong thermal capacity demand window.
stocks.peer_comparison: Adani Power vs JSW Energy, NTPC, Tata Power: APL has highest Q4 PAT growth in the cohort (+64% vs NTPC's mid-single-digit). 95% of operating capacity now tied up under long-term PPAs - highest visibility among private IPPs. Capex pipeline of Rs 2 lakh cr is the most aggressive in the sector. Trades at premium to NTPC on EV/EBITDA but discount to JSW Energy.
red_flags: Adani Group governance overhang persists - Hindenburg legacy and SEBI investigation closed (Sep 2025) but pledged-share/related-party concerns periodically resurface in disclosures
Revenue growth muted (+10% in Q4) vs profit surge - quality of earnings depends on one-off/regulatory items review
Massive Rs 2 lakh cr capex programme adds leverage and execution risk
Adani Group stocks remain in heightened SEBI/exchange surveillance from time to time - check ASM/GSM lists
Promoter pledge levels in Adani Group entities have historically been a flag
catalysts: Q4 FY26 print (April 29) was a strong positive trigger - momentum continuing
Capacity expansion to 41,870 MW by FY32 - new PPA wins are catalysts
Dividend announcement (if any) - record date watch
Possible inclusion in MSCI/Nifty index reviews; FII flow rotation back to Adani complex

stocks.recent_news: Stock surged ~6% in intraday trade on May 4 to Rs 4,578.90 on heavy volumes; up 31% since start of April. Q4 FY26 results scheduled today (May 4) after market hours, with investor meeting on May 16. GE Aerospace delivered the 6th engine for LCA Tejas Mk1A in early April 2026 - reduces critical delivery uncertainty. HAL signed new contract with Pawan Hans for 10 Dhruv NG helicopters and spares, exceeding Rs 1,800 cr in value (delivery by 2027). Order book reportedly exceeds Rs 1,89,300 cr; Maharatna status; Rs 14,000-15,000 cr capex over 5 years for capacity build-out. DAM Capital includes HAL among top defence picks.
stocks.sector_context: Defence sector has structural tailwind: FY27 defence allocation Rs 7.85 lakh cr (Budget 2026-27); Defence indigenisation push; export target Rs 50,000 cr by FY29. Aero engine collaboration ecosystem (GE F404/F414) reducing supply-chain risk for Tejas line. Sector P/E multiples remain elevated (30x+) on multi-decade growth thesis - sentiment-driven element acknowledged by analysts.
stocks.peer_comparison: HAL vs BEL/BDL/Mazagon Dock: HAL has the largest order book (Rs 1.89 lakh cr) but most concentrated execution risk (Tejas/LCH/Dhruv). BEL has more diversified electronics order book and showed 17% revenue growth + 34% PAT growth in FY25 (similar profile expected FY26). HAL stock outperformance (+31% in April) leads PSU defence pack heading into Q4 print.
red_flags: Q4 FY25 base was weak (PAT -7.7%, revenue -7.2%) - YoY comparison may flatter Q4 FY26 print
GE engine delivery cadence remains the single largest execution swing factor for Tejas Mk1A
P/E above 30x already prices in robust order execution - any miss/delay punished sharply
Stock at multi-month high - profit-booking risk on Q4 print today
Sector valuation called sentiment-driven; analyst caution on entry levels
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results today (May 4, 2026) after market hours - immediate event
Investor meeting May 16, 2026 - guidance on order pipeline and Tejas deliveries
Potential interim/final dividend announcement
More Tejas Mk1A engine deliveries from GE; further IAF/MoD orders (Tejas Mk2, AMCA)
Defence export contracts; LUH/LCH order momentum

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results: revenue Rs 2,964.1 cr (+33.7% YoY); operating EBITDA Rs 263.5 cr (+34.6%); PAT Rs 168.0 cr (+30.1%). FY26 revenue Rs 9,722.4 cr (+27.6% YoY) - crossed $1 bn revenue milestone. Wires & Cables (90% of revenue) grew 36.3% YoY in Q4. FMEG segment narrowing losses, targets breakeven by FY27. Final dividend Rs 5.50/share (total Rs 9.50/share for FY26). Management targets 300 bps margin expansion by FY28 (130 bps achieved this year).
stocks.sector_context: India wires & cables market valued at $23.13 bn in 2026, growing at 9.01% CAGR to $35.58 bn by 2031. Motilal Oswal sees market doubling to Rs 1.9 trillion by FY30. Key drivers: infra capex, Rs 300 bn data center opportunity, Rs 8 bn EHV capex, exports. SECTOR HEADWIND: UltraTech Cement entering wires & cables with Rs 18 bn investment (commissioning Dec 2026) - this announcement caused 15% drop across cable stocks earlier. Polycab, KEI, RR Kabel, Havells facing potential margin pressure from new entrant.
stocks.peer_comparison: Market share: Polycab ~18%, KEI ~9%, RR Kabel ~7%. RRKABEL's Q4 revenue growth (+33.7%) outpaced industry growth, indicating share gains. PAT growth +30.1% in line with peers. RRKABEL has stronger consumer-brand wire positioning vs KEI (project-heavy) and Polycab (most diversified). FY26 margin improvement of 130 bps shows operating leverage despite competitive intensity.
red_flags: UltraTech Cement entering wires & cables segment (Rs 18 bn capex, Dec 2026 commissioning) is a structural disruptor for the entire cable peer group
FMEG segment still loss-making - breakeven only targeted in FY27
Copper/aluminium price volatility directly impacts margins
Smaller scale than Polycab/KEI - vulnerability to a price war
Valuation rich (P/E and P/B near upper-end of historical band)
catalysts: Q4 FY26 print (just reported) - record revenue, $1 bn milestone is positive
Final dividend Rs 5.50/share record date upcoming
FY28 margin expansion roadmap (300 bps target) - management commentary
Data center capex acceleration - cable demand catalyst
Government infrastructure spending and electrification orders
